title: The Kedushah: When We Imitate the Angels
title_zh: 圣洁祷文（Kedushah）：当我们模仿天使
title_he: הקדושה: כאשר אנחנו מחקים את המלאכים
description: Rising on our toes, swaying in unison, crying "Kadosh, Kadosh, Kadosh" — what happens during Kedushah and its mystical significance.
author: Torah Light Content Team
chinese_parallels: Confucian Li (礼) - ritual propriety and cosmic order
Daoist Dao (道) - transcendent yet immanent ultimate reality
Buddhist group chanting and meditation practices
Celestial hierarchies in Chinese folk religion
historical_context: Isaiah's vision (Spring and Autumn Period, 春秋时代, ~770-476 BCE)
Ezekiel's vision (Late Spring and Autumn Period, 春秋时代晚期, ~586 BCE)
Zohar composition (Late Song Dynasty, 宋末, ~1290 CE)
Shulchan Aruch compilation (Ming Dynasty, 明朝, 16th century)
key_concepts: Kedushah - Holiness prayer imitating angelic praise
Kadosh, Kadosh, Kadosh - Holy, Holy, Holy
Seraphim and Chayot HaKodesh - Categories of angels
Minyan requirement - Ten-person quorum for communal prayer
Physical actions - Rising on toes, swaying, feet together
learning_objectives: Understand the biblical sources for Kedushah in Isaiah and Ezekiel
Learn the structure and meaning of the Kedushah prayer
Recognize the physical actions and their symbolic significance
Appreciate the mystical dimension of joining the angelic realm
Understand why Kedushah requires a minyan (community)
Compare Jewish concepts of holiness with Chinese philosophical traditions
Apply principles of aspiration and elevation to daily spiritual practice
